page: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Edsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It also includes 118 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU test shows average performance with a score of 2215.58, whereas the multi-core CPU performance is categorized as weak at 2263.98. Memory bandwidth tests reveal weak read-only performance, measuring 168.1 GB/sec for small blocks and 22.92 GB/sec for large blocks, alongside a poor write performance of 12.15 GB/sec for large blocks. PassMark metrics align with these findings, showing a weak overall CPU score of 4263.53 but an average memory score of 1927.71.

The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. In LLM inference benchmarks using a small 135M model, the server achieves an average text generation speed of 88.94 tokens/second, though prompt processing remains weak at 288.44 tokens/second. Redis SET operations reach 4.01 million operations/second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 4.95 GB/sec, both falling into the weak performance category.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the E2ds_v7 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The inclusion of 118 GB of local storage and Premium Storage capability makes it suitable for small-scale databases, caching layers, and lightweight enterprise applications. However, the single physical core limits its suitability for highly parallelized compute-intensive tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs on a single physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C core operating at 4.2 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of RAM, 118 GB of local storage, and supports Premium Storage. While single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores are average, multi-core performance and uncached memory write bandwidth are weak. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enabling average text generation speeds of 88.94 tokens/second on small LLM models. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads such as small databases, caching systems, and lightweight enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 118 GB of local storage, designed for memory-bound workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, high memory-to-core density, and integrated local storage for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
16.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory allocation
118 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support for specialized workloads
Best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications
